description: Comprehensive guide to C++ syntax fundamentals: program structure, output/input, comments, and basic statements. Every concept includes definition and working code.

sections.title: Program Structure
sections.definition: A C++ program consists of header includes, a main() function (entry point), and optional namespaces. Execution starts at main().

sections.title: Output with cout
sections.definition: cout sends data to the console. Use << to chain items. endl adds a newline and flushes output; \n is just a newline.

sections.title: Input with cin
sections.definition: cin reads input from the user. Use >> to store values. Multiple inputs can be chained.

sections.title: Comments
sections.definition: Comments are ignored by the compiler. Use // for single-line and /* */ for multi-line comments.

sections.title: Statements and Blocks
sections.definition: A statement is an instruction ending with a semicolon. A block is a group of statements inside curly braces {}.

sections.title: Basic Variable Declaration and Initialization
sections.definition: Variables must be declared with a type before use. C++11 introduced uniform initialization using {}.

sections.title: Arithmetic Operators
sections.definition: Operators: + - * / % (modulus). Integer division truncates. Use parentheses for grouping.
sections.output:
a + b = 13
a - b = 7
a * b = 30
a / b = 3 (integer division)
a % b = 1
x / y = 3.33333

sections.title: Increment and Decrement Operators
sections.definition: ++ increments by 1, -- decrements by 1. Prefix (++x) changes then uses; postfix (x++) uses then changes.
